mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals increasing factory output and strong economic activity, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines month-over-month and SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and falling consumer income, which depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 6-month window
regime_hint: early recession or labor market deterioration
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises >0.5pp over 6 months but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated and inflation remains high, the joint tightening of monetary policy compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If both trigger conditions are met but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such episodes over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-2008 recovery phase, rising real retail sales reflect strengthening consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS grows month-over-month and SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such months during the identified recovery period (2009-2012).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in housing starts signals strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which quickly lifts equity market sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If HOUST rises >5% mom but SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such events over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP indicates sustainable economic growth that raises corporate profits and drives equity prices higher over a quarterly horizon, reflecting the fundamental valuation channel.
failure_condition: If real GDP growth is negative quarter-over-quarter and SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such quarters over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment and a decline in the unemployment rate provide a strong signal of a tightening labor market, raising wage and consumption expectations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0 and UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, stable consumer prices reduce uncertainty about future monetary policy and preserve real purchasing power, which supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is below 2% but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of observed quarters over a 5-year rolling window.
